figures.name: Jah Rastafari (Emperor Haile Selassie I)
figures.gloss: The Supreme Being incarnate in Emperor Haile Selassie I of Ethiopia. He is the living God of the Black diaspora, the conquering Lion of the Tribe of Judah, who brings redemption to the oppressed and sits on the throne of King David.
figures.facet: Unlike the white, sky-dwelling God of colonial Christianity, Jah is a living, Black African monarch of the modern era. His divinity is not deferred to an afterlife; it is immanent and historically verified.
figures.provenance: The Holy Piby; Kebra Nagast; oral teachings of early preachers like Leonard Howell.

figures.name: Babylon
figures.gloss: The oppressive, materialistic, and corrupt western power structure, originally embodied by colonial Britain and the papacy, but encompassing the entire global capitalist system. It is the spiritual and physical site of Black exile and systemic bondage.
figures.facet: It is not a mythological underworld or a spiritual demon, but a present sociopolitical reality—a state machinery of police, politicians, and economic exploitation that must be actively resisted and fled.
figures.provenance: Psalms 137; Revelation; reggae lyricism; foundational Rastafari reasoning sessions.

figures.name: Zion (Africa / Ethiopia)
figures.gloss: The sacred homeland and spiritual epicenter for all Black people, geographically located in Ethiopia and the broader African continent. It represents ultimate liberation, natural living, and the promised land of return where systemic oppression ceases.
figures.facet: Zion is not a post-mortem heavenly paradise but a tangible, geographical continent. Salvation means physical repatriation to African soil in this lifetime, violently rejecting the colonial promise of heaven after death.
figures.provenance: The Kebra Nagast; Hebrew Bible; Garveyite pan-African discourse.

figures.name: Marcus Garvey
figures.gloss: The pioneering Pan-African leader revered as a major prophet. He prophesied the crowning of a Black king in Africa, laying the ideological and spiritual groundwork for the movement and sparking the global consciousness of Black repatriation.
figures.facet: Garvey is venerated despite his own historical rejection of Rastafari. His political speeches and Black nationalist organizing were retroactively canonized as infallible divine prophecy by the early Rastafari patriarchs.
figures.provenance: Universal Negro Improvement Association (UNIA) speeches; oral traditions of early Rastafari.

figures.name: I-and-I (I-n-I)
figures.gloss: The core philosophical concept of divine unity. It expresses the unbroken, immanent presence of Jah within every individual, destroying the boundary between the human subject and the divine creator, and affirming radical equality among all living people.
figures.facet: It abolishes the grammatical and spiritual subjugation of 'you' and 'me'. By speaking 'I and I', the Rastaman constantly affirms that God is not separate from the self, disrupting colonial hierarchies of worth.
figures.provenance: Rastafari reasoning sessions (groundations); Dread Talk (Iyaric) linguistic philosophy.

figures.name: The Holy Herb (Ganja)
figures.gloss: The sacred marijuana plant, viewed as the 'weed of wisdom' grown on the tomb of King Solomon. It is smoked as a religious sacrament to burn away corruption, facilitate deep meditation, and commune directly with Jah.
figures.facet: Unlike a symbolic ritual wafer or wine, ganja is a psychoactive catalyst for 'reasoning'. It is explicitly an illegal, criminalized plant that physically alters consciousness to break the mental programming of Babylon.
figures.provenance: Genesis 1:29; Revelation 22:2; Indo-Jamaican cultural exchange; Rastafari oral tradition.

figures.name: Nyabinghi
figures.gloss: The foundational, heartbeat-rhythm drumming, chanting, and spiritual gathering of Rastafari. Named after a legendary Ugandan warrior queen, it is an invocation of divine justice designed to destroy oppression and Babylon through sheer acoustic and spiritual force.
figures.facet: It merges music, theology, and anti-colonial warfare. Nyabinghi is not merely worship; it is an active sonic weapon, where the rhythmic heartbeat is believed to physically and spiritually dismantle the walls of Babylon.
figures.provenance: East African anti-colonial history (Muhumusa); Jamaican Burru drumming; Mansions of Rastafari traditions.

figures.name: Queen Omega (Empress Menen Asfaw)
figures.gloss: The divine feminine counterpart to Jah, incarnate in Empress Menen Asfaw of Ethiopia. She represents the Mother of Creation, the balancing root of the earth, and the cosmic archetype of the righteous Black woman.
figures.facet: While the movement can be highly patriarchal, Queen Omega establishes an inescapable divine dyad. King Alpha cannot rule without Queen Omega, rooting salvation in a married, historical African partnership rather than a solitary male deity.
figures.provenance: The Royal Parchment Scroll of Black Supremacy; The Promised Key (Leonard Howell).

edges.note: Jah represents ultimate life, freedom, and divine truth, standing in total systemic opposition to the death and bondage of Babylon.
King Alpha and Queen Omega form the supreme divine dyad, demonstrating that perfect rule requires balanced male and female energies.
GOLD SEAM: Jah is a direct linguistic and theological inheritance of the Biblical Yahweh, re-contextualized into a living Black messiah.
GOLD SEAM: Haile Selassie is explicitly worshipped as the returned Christ in his Kingly character, fulfilling biblical prophecy.
GOLD SEAM: Both represent an oppressive, illegitimate architectural system designed to trap divine sparks in a realm of suffering and illusion.
GOLD SEAM: A pervasive, illusion-generating force that binds humanity to a corrupt material cycle, which must be overcome through enlightenment.
GOLD SEAM: The primordial, uncorrupted paradise of human origin, envisioned not just as a mythic past but a geographic destiny.
GOLD SEAM: The absolute spiritual and physical center of the universe, serving as the orienting homeland for the righteous.
GOLD SEAM: The localized fragment of the supreme creator dwelling directly within the human vessel, overriding worldly oppression.
GOLD SEAM: The supreme, unifying reality that dissolves the illusion of separation between the self and the cosmos.
GOLD SEAM: While Buddhism dissolves the self into non-self to end suffering, Rastafari elevates the self into the absolute 'I' to conquer oppression.
GOLD SEAM: The essential divine feminine consort who anchors and completes the supreme male deity in a cosmic partnership.
GOLD SEAM: A physiological and communal technology used to enter an ecstatic state, heal the community, and contact the divine.
GOLD SEAM: The prophetic forerunner who heralds the imminent arrival of a final, triumphant divine order that will remake the world.
GOLD SEAM: The overarching system of total negativity, destruction, and spiritual falsehood that wages war against the forces of light.
GOLD SEAM: A rhythmic, communal ritual meant to re-align the community with cosmic justice and ancestral truth.

proposedArchetypes.name: The Sacred Medicine
proposedArchetypes.gloss: A psychoactive plant, fungus, or brew consumed as a direct, chemical sacrament to dissolve worldly illusions and commune with the divine.
proposedArchetypes.reason: Crucial for mapping traditions where an organic psychoactive substance is not just a symbol, but the literal engine of spiritual revelation and divine contact (e.g., Ganja, Soma, Ayahuasca).
